source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
title: Ensure management console sign-in without MFA is monitored

input_row.source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure management console sign-in without MFA is monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established for console logins that are not protected by multi-factor authentication (MFA).
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Monitoring for single-factor console logins will increase visibility into accounts that are not protected by MFA. These type of accounts are more susceptible to compromise and unauthorized access.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrail trails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> o ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> o Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group- name> 3. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: "filterPattern": "{ ($.eventName = "ConsoleLogin") && ($.additionalEventData.MFAUsed != "Yes") }" Or, to reduce false positives in case Single Sign-On (SSO) is used in the organization: "filterPattern": "{ ($.eventName = "ConsoleLogin") && ($.additionalEventData.MFAUsed != "Yes") && ($.userIdentity.type = "IAMUser") && ($.responseElements.ConsoleLogin = "Success") }" 4. Note the <no-mfa-console-signin-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 5. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <no-mfa-console-signin- metric> captured in step 4. aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query 'MetricAlarms[?MetricName== <no- mfa-console-signin-metric>]' 6. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 7. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for AWS Management Console sign-ins without MFA and uses the <trail-log-group- name> taken from audit step 1. aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> -- filter-name `<no-mfa-console-signin-metric>` --metric-transformations metricName= `<no-mfa-console-signin- metric>`,metricNamespace='CISBenchmark',metricValue=1 --filter-pattern '{ ($.eventName = "ConsoleLogin") && ($.additionalEventData.MFAUsed != "Yes") }' Or, to reduce false positives in case Single Sign-On (SSO) is used in the organization: aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> -- filter-name `<no-mfa-console-signin-metric>` --metric-transformations metricName= `<no-mfa-console-signin- metric>`,metricNamespace='CISBenchmark',metricValue=1 --filter-pattern '{ ($.eventName = "ConsoleLogin") && ($.additionalEventData.MFAUsed != "Yes") && ($.userIdentity.type = "IAMUser") && ($.responseElements.ConsoleLogin = "Success") }' Note : You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 2. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note : You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note : Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 3. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note : You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. 4. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name `<no-mfa-console-signin- alarm>` --metric-name `<no-mfa-console-signin-metric>` --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --namespace 'CISBenchmark' --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn> References: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/DeveloperGuide/viewin g_metrics_with_cloudwatch.html 2. CCE-79187-1 3. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/receive-cloudtrail- log-files-from-multiple-regions.html 4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudwatch-alarms- for-cloudtrail.html 5. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/SubscribeTopic.html Additional Information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored Filter pattern set to { ($.eventName = "ConsoleLogin") && ($.additionalEventData.MFAUsed != "Yes") && ($.userIdentity.type = "IAMUser") && ($.responseElements.ConsoleLogin = "Success"}: • reduces false alarms raised when a user logs in via SSO
input_row.references: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/DeveloperGuide/viewin g_metrics_with_cloudwatch.html 2. CCE-79187-1 3. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/receive-cloudtrail- log-files-from-multiple-regions.html 4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awscloudtrail/latest/userguide/cloudwatch-alarms- for-cloudtrail.html 5. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sns/latest/dg/SubscribeTopic.html Additional Information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored Filter pattern set to { ($.eventName = "ConsoleLogin") && ($.additionalEventData.MFAUsed != "Yes") && ($.userIdentity.type = "IAMUser") && ($.responseElements.ConsoleLogin = "Success"}: • reduces false alarms raised when a user logs in via SSO

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control is primarily focused on ensuring that management console sign-ins without MFA are monitored using AWS CloudTrail and CloudWatch, which are standard AWS services with well-documented APIs. This control involves checking the configuration of these services, which can be fully verified using the AWS CLI and/or boto3 SDK.

- **AWS APIs can query this property:** Yes, through CloudTrail and CloudWatch APIs.
- **Standard AWS service property:** Yes, relates to logging and monitoring with CloudTrail and CloudWatch.
- **Boto3 SDK methods:** Yes, methods are available to check CloudTrail status and CloudWatch metric filters and alarms.
- **AWS CLI commands:** Provided in audit section, confirming these checks can be automated.
- **AWS Services involved:** CloudTrail and CloudWatch, which are directly queryable via APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_console_login_mfa_monitoring_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program would perform the following checks:

1. **Enumerate CloudTrail Trails:**
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails()` to list all trails.
   - Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails where `IsMultiRegionTrail` is set to `true`.

2. **Verify Trail Configuration:**
   - Ensure each identified trail is active by calling `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_trail_status(Name=<trail_name>)` and checking that `IsLogging` is `TRUE`.
   - Confirm that management events are captured by using `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_event_selectors(TrailName=<trail_name>)` to ensure `IncludeManagementEvents` is set to `true` and `ReadWriteType` is `All`.

3. **Check Metric Filters:**
   - Retrieve metric filters for the log group associated with the trail using `boto3.client('logs').describe_metric_filters(logGroupName=<trail-log-group-name>)`.
   - Ensure a metric filter exists with a pattern that detects console logins without MFA.

4. **Verify CloudWatch Alarms:**
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms()` to list alarms.
   - Confirm alarms are configured for the metric identified for console logins without MFA (filter based on `MetricName`).

5. **Verify SNS Topic and Subscription:**
   - Use `boto3.client('sns').list_subscriptions_by_topic(TopicArn=<sns-topic-arn>)` to verify there is an active subscriber to the SNS topic that the CloudWatch alarm notifies.

6. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - Pass if there is an active, multi-region CloudTrail capturing all management events, a CloudWatch Logs metric filter detecting console logins without MFA, a corresponding CloudWatch alarm, and active SNS topic with a subscriber.
   - Fail if any of these components are missing or misconfigured.

This automation ensures comprehensive monitoring of console sign-ins without MFA across all AWS regions, enhancing visibility and compliance with CIS Benchmark standards.
step2_review:
### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: AGREE

- **Manual vs Automated Classification:** The classification as "Automated" is correct. The control can be fully verified using AWS APIs, boto3, and AWS CLI, which allows for programmatic checks of the configurations in question.
- **AWS APIs/boto3/CLI:** The assessment correctly identifies that AWS APIs, boto3, and AWS CLI can be used to automate the verification of this control. The necessary methods and commands are available to check CloudTrail, CloudWatch, and SNS configurations.

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: 9

- **Automation Details:** The automation details are technically accurate. The boto3 methods and AWS CLI commands mentioned are valid and appropriate for the tasks described.
- **Boto3 Methods/CLI Commands:** The methods such as `describe_trails`, `get_trail_status`, `get_event_selectors`, `describe_metric_filters`, `describe_alarms`, and `list_subscriptions_by_topic` are correctly used for their respective purposes.
- **Manual Steps:** There are no manual steps required, as the automation covers all necessary checks.
- **Program Name:** The program name `aws_cloudtrail_console_login_mfa_monitoring_enabled` is descriptive but could be more specific. It should reflect the specific security check being performed, such as `aws_cloudtrail_console_login_without_mfa_monitoring`.

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS:

- **Program Name:** Consider renaming the program to `aws_cloudtrail_console_login_without_mfa_monitoring` to clearly indicate the specific security check being performed.
- **Additional Considerations:** Ensure that the automation script includes error handling for cases where no trails, metric filters, or alarms are found, to avoid false negatives.
- **Alternative Methods:** Consider using AWS Config rules to complement the automation. AWS Config can provide continuous monitoring and compliance checks for CloudTrail and CloudWatch configurations.

### 4. CONFIDENCE: HIGH

- The assessment is well-structured and technically sound. The use of AWS APIs and boto3 methods is appropriate and comprehensive for the control being audited.

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: Modify

- **Modify:** Update the program name to `aws_cloudtrail_console_login_without_mfa_monitoring` for clarity and specificity.
- **Keep Automation:** The automated approach is appropriate and should be maintained.
- **Consider AWS Config:** As an additional improvement, consider integrating AWS Config rules to provide continuous compliance monitoring alongside the automated checks.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that this control can be fully verified using AWS APIs, specifically via boto3 or AWS CLI, as all relevant properties (CloudTrail multi-region status, CloudWatch metric filters, alarms for console logins without MFA) are exposed and queryable. No manual inspection or console-only steps are required; all audit and remediation steps can be programmatically validated. The only minor disagreement was on the program name, not the approach.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
aws_cloudtrail_console_login_without_mfa_monitoring

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS Resource/Service:** CloudTrail Trails, CloudWatch Log Groups, CloudWatch Metric Filters, CloudWatch Alarms, SNS Topics (optional for notifications)
- **API/CLI Methods:**  
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').describe_trails()`  
  - `boto3.client('cloudtrail').get_trail_status(Name=...)`  
  - `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_metric_filters(logGroupName=...)`  
  - `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms()`  
  - (Optionally) `boto3.client('sns').list_subscriptions_by_topic()`
- **Properties to Check:**  
  - At least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail (`IsMultiRegionTrail: True`, `IsLogging: True`)
  - CloudTrail trail is logging management events
  - CloudTrail logs are sent to a CloudWatch Log Group
  - CloudWatch Log Group has a metric filter for console logins without MFA (filter pattern: `{($.eventName = "ConsoleLogin") && ($.additionalEventData.MFAUsed != "Yes")}`)
  - CloudWatch Alarm exists for the above metric filter and is enabled
- **Pass Criteria:**  
  - There is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with management events logging enabled.
  - The associated CloudWatch Log Group has a metric filter for console logins without MFA.
  - There is an enabled CloudWatch Alarm for this metric filter.
- **Fail Criteria:**  
  - No active multi-region CloudTrail trail.
  - No metric filter for console logins without MFA on the relevant log group.
  - No enabled CloudWatch Alarm for this metric filter.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
Both AI opinions agreed on automation. The only difference was the program name; I selected the more specific and descriptive name as suggested by the second opinion.

**SOURCE:** CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
